message:
test(fuzz): add ProvenanceRecord::merge MIN/MAX/OR rules fuzz target (resolves hpgza)

ProvenanceRecord::merge (provenance.rs:457-506) is the SECURITY
CRITICAL combine: integrity = MIN (lowest wins, most conservative);
confidentiality = MAX (highest wins, most restrictive); taint = OR
(union). Doc-comment explicitly: "This prevents 'mix trusted with
untrusted' from upgrading trust."

A regression that swapped MIN↔MAX or dropped a taint flag would break
the trust-flow lattice silently. Existing taint_flags fuzz (k1qyp)
covers TaintFlags::merge but NOT the integrity+confidentiality+taint
composition.

Six properties driven over a u8-bitmask of 8 TaintFlag variants and
discriminator-driven integrity/confidentiality/zone:
- empty input → current_zone default
- MIN-integrity: result == min(records.integrity)
- MAX-confidentiality: result == max(records.confidentiality)
- OR-taint per-flag: result.contains(f) ⇔ any record contains f
- current_zone preserved (not overridden by record origins)
- single-record identity: labels and taint preserved verbatim

Once-gated regression anchors:
- (a) merge([Owner, Untrusted]) → Untrusted (MIN; trust-mixing
  prevention) verified order-independent
- (b) merge([Public, Owner]) → Owner (MAX; restrictive flows up)
- (c) merge([+PublicInput, untainted]) → result has PublicInput
  (OR-union, trust upgrade via taint stripping prevented)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
